title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Automation and Data Client Systems · Code Recall
summary: A compliance analyst reconstructing a partial ingestion handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for cookies, credential modes, api keys, bearer tokens, and secret hygiene, producing least-authority credential and session policy. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers least-authority credential and session policy after a delayed changed case. Lear…
objectives: Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Recognize ambient cookie authority and require CSRF defenses for state-changing browser requests. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Place API keys in approved headers or signing inputs and exclude them from URLs, source, telemetry, and error text. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Credential Provider Design
steps.instruction:
review case — compliance handoff automation handles host-only session cookie SID-5, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently.
